seoTitle: Import Invoices From a CSV File Into QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Batch-upload customer invoices into QuickBooks Online from a CSV or Excel file, mapping columns and fixing errors before the final import.
intro: Manually entering a large batch of customer invoices wastes hours and invites typos into your accounts receivable. QuickBooks Online's spreadsheet import wizard lets you upload a CSV file, map the columns to the right fields, and catch errors before anything posts to your books.

steps.heading: Open the Import Data tool
steps.body: Click the Gear icon in the upper-right corner and select Import Data, then choose the Invoices tile.

steps.heading: Upload your CSV file
steps.body: Click Browse to select your local spreadsheet, or download the sample file first to match Intuit's preferred date and formatting layout.

steps.heading: Map your columns
steps.body: Click Next and match each QBO field, such as Invoice No, Customer, and Amount, to the corresponding column headers in your CSV.

steps.heading: Set the date format
steps.body: Use the date format dropdown to ensure the import engine interprets your transactional rows correctly.

steps.heading: Review, fix, and import
steps.body: Check the data grid for red error flags or blank rows, fix any typos directly in the table cells, then click Import to run the batch.

faq.question: Where do I get a template that matches QuickBooks' expected format?
faq.answer: Click Download a sample file during the upload step to see Intuit's preferred column layout and formatting.

faq.question: What happens if a row has an error?
faq.answer: The review screen flags problem rows in red, and you can click directly into any cell to correct the data before finishing the import.

faq.question: Will importing duplicate customer records overwrite existing ones?
faq.answer: Correctly configuring customer matching behavior during setup helps prevent overwriting pre-existing profiles.

faq.question: How do I verify the import worked correctly?
faq.answer: Open your Customer Invoices directory afterward and run a customer balance summary report to confirm the batch registered accurately.
